Notice Of Motion, Motion And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents And Things, Set Two And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-08-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Andrew D. Weaver, Esq Scott Cole & Assocaites, Telephone 925-355-5200, appearing for Plaintiff;
Ed Clements, Esq., Telephone 408-835-3270, appearing for Defendant Legion Corporation;

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFS RAMONA SCOTT, RAMON SCOTT'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents And Things, Set Two And Request For Sanctions

Granted. Defendant Legion shall serve a complete amended response, without objections, within 30 days of service of this order. (The Court does find that Legion's response to demand for production, served by mail January 22, was timely served.) Under Pioneer Electronics v. Sup. Ct. (2007) 40 Cal.4th 360, 373, it is settled law that plaintiffs are entitled to discover the identities and contact information of other potential class members. However, because employee records are sought, there is a privacy issue, subject to the Court's balancing the parties' discovery needs vs. privacy. Legion was thus not without substantial justification in refusing to produce the records without a court order. Sanctions denied.

Order is signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Charles Geerhart; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff Ramon Scotts Further Responses To Form Interrogatories Set 1 And Monetary Sanction Request Against Plaintiff
Off Calendar Jan-02-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Andrew D. Weaver, Esq., Scott Cole & Assocaites, Telephone (925) 355-5200, appearing for Plaintiff;
E. J. Clements, Esq., Telephone (408) 835-3778, appearing for Defendant;

RULING: ARGUED. The Court makes the following ruling:

DEFENDANT LEGION CORPORATION'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff Ramon Scotts Further Responses To Form Interrogatories Set 1 And Monetary Sanction Request Against Plaintiff. Defendants motion is denied with respect to the following form interrogatories: 2.6, 2.7, 12.1, 12.2, 12.3, 12.4, 12.5, 12.6, 12.7, 14.1, 14.2. The use of these interrogatories with the default definition of the word incident is inappropriate and, in the case of several of the interrogatories, their use is inappropriate for this kind of case. Furthermore, for the interrogatories relating to personal information of the plaintiff (2.6; 2.7), the separate statement does not adequately establish the need for of the requested information.

Plaintiff's motion is granted with respect to form interrogatory 2.5 and 17.1. The objection with regard to the term "incident" highlights the boilerplate nature of the objections asserted in plaintiffs' responses throughout all of the responses at issue in these motions. The word "incident" does not appear in 17.1. The interrogatory is crystal-clear in that each subpart must be answered for each request for admission that is not an "unqualified admission." Notwithstanding this clarity, plaintiffs decline to respond to parts b - d stating only that it is "not applicable."
Fully code compliant, verified responses must be served for form interrogatory 2.5 and 17.1 within 15 days of the date of the Order. Sanctions are not awarded to either party for lines 3 and 6.

The moving party shall prepare a Proposed Order consistent with this ruling and submit to plaintiffs' counsel per CRC 3.1312. (If the parties make an agreement wherein defendant waives sanctions, they do not have to be included in the order.) After CRC 3.1312 has been complied with, the Proposed Order should be emailed to this email address with opposing counsel copied on any emails: Judge Pro Tem Greg Groeneveld at greg@greggroeneveldlaw.com

Judge Pro Tem: Greg Groeneveld; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff Ramon Scotts Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set 1, And Monetary Sanction Request Against Plaintiff
Off Calendar Jan-02-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Andrew D. Weaver, Esq., Scott Cole & Assocaites, Telephone (925) 355-5200, appearing for Plaintiff;
E. J. Clements, Esq., Telephone (408) 835-3778, appearing for Defendant;

RULING: ARGUED. The Court makes the following ruling:

DEFENDANT LEGION CORPORATION'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff Ramon Scotts Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set 1, And Monetary Sanction Request Against Plaintiff. Defendant's motion is granted with respect to all special interrogatories at issue in the motion. Specifically:

Interrogatories directed to Meal Period Claims by Plaintiff: Interrogatories number 1, 2, 10, 11, 12;
Interrogatories directed to Wage Loss Claims by Plaintiff. Interrogatories number 3, 4, 5, 6;
Interrogatories directed to Rest Period Claims by Plaintiff. Interrogatories number 7, 8;

Fully code compliant, verified responses must be served within 15 days of the date of the Order. Plaintiffs shall pay defendant sanctions in the amount of $1,090 for failure to make discovery within 15 days of the date of the order. Each plaintiff shall be responsible for 50% of the total sanctions. Plaintiffs' request for sanctions is denied.
Sanctions are appropriate on a number of grounds. California Code of Civil Procedure 2023.010 provides a number of grounds for sanctions which are applicable here. The almost complete lack of response to clearly relevant interrogatories is sanctionable as a failure to respond (2023.010 (d)) and the unreasonable reliance on frivolous, boilerplate objections is sanctionable. 2023.010 (e).

There is some lack of clarity over whether the October 31, 2018 letter was received and the sanctions awarded have been reduced by $500 to account for that. However, Exhibit C to defendant's declaration is an adequate meet and confer for this situation and no more should have been required to elicit better responses. The declaration submitted in the opposition paperwork mentions, at paragraph a "long discussion" on October 31, 2018. It is difficult to understand why more than that was needed as to the interrogatory responses. Most responses are patently inadequate and their deficiencies are obvious. The objections were boilerplate and unreasonable and no attempt was made to provide anything approaching code compliant responses to interrogatories that go to the heart of plaintiffs' claims. Moving party is to prepare and submit form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312 by email to Judge Pro Tem Greg Groeneveld at greg@greggroeneveldlaw.com

Plaintiffs shall pay total sanctions in the amount of $590 for failure to make discovery within 15 days of the date of the order. Each plaintiff shall be responsible for 50% of the total sanctions. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff Ramona Scotts Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set 1, And Monetary Sanction Request Against Plaintiff
Off Calendar Jan-02-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Andrew D. Weaver, Esq., Scott Cole & Assocaites, Telephone (925) 355-5200, appearing for Plaintiff;
E. J. Clements, Esq., Telephone (408) 835-3778, appearing for Defendant;

RULING: ARGUED. The Court makes the following ruling:

DEFENDANT LEGION CORPORATION'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff Ramona Scotts Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set 1, And Monetary Sanction Request Against Plaintiff. Defendants motion is denied with respect to the following form interrogatories: 2.6, 2.7, 12.1, 12.2, 12.3, 12.4, 12.5, 12.6, 12.7, 14.1, 14.2. The use of these interrogatories with the default definition of the word incident is inappropriate and, in the case of several of the interrogatories, their use is inappropriate for this kind of case. Furthermore, for the interrogatories relating to personal information of the plaintiff (2.6; 2.7), the separate statement does not adequately establish the need for of the requested information.

Plaintiff's motion is granted with respect to form interrogatory 2.5 and 17.1. The objection with regard to the term "incident" highlights the boilerplate nature of the objections asserted in plaintiffs' responses throughout all of the responses at issue in these motions. The word "incident" does not appear in 17.1. The interrogatory is crystal-clear in that each subpart must be answered for each request for admission that is not an "unqualified admission." Notwithstanding this clarity, plaintiffs decline to respond to parts b - d stating only that it is "not applicable."

Fully code compliant, verified responses must be served for form interrogatory 2.5 and 17.1 within 15 days of the date of the Order. Sanctions are not awarded to either party for lines 3 and 6.

The moving party shall prepare a Proposed Order consistent with this ruling and submit to plaintiffs' counsel per CRC 3.1312. (If the parties make an agreement wherein defendant waives sanctions, they do not have to be included in the order.) After CRC 3.1312 has been complied with, the Proposed Order should be emailed to this email address with opposing counsel copied on any emails: to Judge Pro Tem Greg Groeneveld at greg@greggroeneveldlaw.com
